title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 - 12 - 2018: Google Earthing Roman Coloseum

enrichment.summary: Kyle recounts excitedly discovering the 'Rome Reborn' 3D model layer in Google Earth after a late night of work, then walks through its limitations (floating buildings, no ground-level walking, slow loading, all-white Colosseum) compared to his own ongoing ancient Rome reconstruction project in a simpler 3D program. He explains why he still sees value in continuing his own version and shares broader ambitions to map colonized Roman towns using a book on Roman agrarian land laws.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Roman history and republic vs empire
Roman colonial towns and land laws

stances.position: Impressive achievement but too limited for his use case — buildings float, can't be downloaded, no interior access, poor performance
Still worth continuing because it offers transparency, interior views, color-coded data, and works across all scales from aerial to ground level
The Republic era is the most interesting — more democratic, expansionist, exciting history; the Empire is boring and corrupt
Should be $70 not $700; Google should give it to schools and kids to crowdsource historical reconstructions
They did a good job but lack the awning/ropes on the Colosseum and don't take advantage of terrain contours

entities: Roman Agrarian Land Laws of the Republic (book)
